records.name: Pet Resource Center of Kansas City (PRCKC) - Pet Food Pantry
records.free_claim: Site states it provides pet food at no cost to qualifying individuals; described as a supplement for people struggling to feed their pets for the month.
records.nonprofit_claim: Site identifies PRCKC as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.
records.what: Monthly pet food pantry providing free dog and cat food to pet owners in need; membership requires a completed application submitted by email or in person.

records.name: Humane Society of Greater Kansas City - Community Pet Food Pantry
records.free_claim: Site states the pet food pantry is open to anyone and everyone that needs help feeding their pets; described as a barrier-free food pantry, clients can come every 2 weeks with no limit on length of use.
records.nonprofit_claim: Described as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit animal shelter; GuideStar profile EIN 48-0581965.
records.what: Barrier-free community pet food pantry open 7 days a week; dog and cat food for anyone needing help feeding their pets. Call or email to schedule pickup; transportation assistance available.

records.name: Jewish Family Services (JFS) Food Pantry - Jasmine's Corner (Brookside / Kansas City MO)
records.free_claim: Site states food for cats and dogs is available from Jasmine's Corner so pet owners don't have to forgo eating to keep their pets; provided through the free food pantry.
records.nonprofit_claim: Registered nonprofit; GuideStar/IRS EIN 44-0545829.
records.what: Food pantry offering free pet food (Jasmine's Corner) alongside human food; open by appointment to registered clients.

records.name: Jewish Family Services (JFS) Food Pantry - Jasmine's Corner (Overland Park KS)
records.free_claim: Site states food for cats and dogs is available from Jasmine's Corner through the free food pantry at both the Kansas and Missouri locations.
records.nonprofit_claim: Registered nonprofit; GuideStar/IRS EIN 44-0545829.
records.what: Food pantry offering free pet food (Jasmine's Corner) alongside human food; open by appointment to registered clients.

records.name: KC Pet Project - Pet Resource Assistance (Emergency Pet Food)
records.free_claim: Site states it can help with basic pet care resources including emergency pet food, contingent on donations received and approval of a Pet Resource Application.
records.nonprofit_claim: KC Pet Project is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit animal welfare organization (operates Kansas City MO's municipal shelter); page references board review by GKCCF and donation-funded structure.
records.what: Emergency/free pet food and basic pet care resources for pet owners in need, provided by application (Pet Resource Request form), subject to donation availability; serves the Kansas City area.

records.name: KC Shepherd's Center - Meals on Wheels Pet Food Program
records.free_claim: Site states that since 2013 it has delivered free pet food and supplies to clients, partnering with area animal welfare organizations who donate food and supplies, after learning clients shared their meals with pets.
records.nonprofit_claim: 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, EIN 43-0994417.
records.what: Meals on Wheels program for homebound adults 60+ or disabled that also delivers free pet food and supplies. By application via the program manager. Serves Jackson, Platte, Cass, Clay Counties (MO) and Johnson County (KS); also cited as serving Wyandotte County KS.

records.name: Bi-State Pet Food Pantry
records.what: Volunteer-run nonprofit providing emergency/supplemental pet food assistance to keep pets with their families during financial hardship. Distributes at scheduled distribution dates/times; serves the greater St. Louis community. Assisted 2,000+ pets and distributed 155,000+ lbs of food last year.
records.free_claim: Free emergency pet food assistance to families experiencing financial hardship (per organization site).
records.nonprofit_claim: Volunteer-run nonprofit pet food pantry serving the St. Louis community; listed as a nonprofit on GreatNonprofits and the Missouri Coalition of Animal Care Organizations resource page.

records.name: Open Door Animal Sanctuary - Pet Food Pantry
records.what: Nonprofit animal sanctuary with a walk-in pet food pantry offering free pet food (and, depending on availability, toys, leashes, and beds) to local residents in need. Open Monday-Friday 10am-4pm; no documentation needed. Serves more than 1,000 families annually.
records.free_claim: Free pet food pantry; walk in Mon-Fri 10am-4pm, no documentation needed (per findhelp.org listing).
records.nonprofit_claim: Open Door Animal Sanctuary is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit (GuideStar profile 23-7444249).

records.name: Jefferson County Pet Food Pantry (Animal Buddies of Missouri)
records.what: 100% volunteer-based 501(c)(3) providing free pet food and spay/neuter assistance to low-income families, seniors, disabled, and homeless pet owners in Jefferson County and surrounding rural counties. Distribution on the 3rd Saturday of each month, 10am-12pm.
records.free_claim: Provides free pet food once a month to low-income families, seniors, disabled, and homeless pet owners (per org site).
records.nonprofit_claim: 501(c)(3), 100% volunteer-based organization relying solely on donations (dba Animal Buddies of Missouri).

records.name: Five Acres Animal Shelter - AniMeals Pet Food Pantry
records.what: Nonprofit animal shelter pet food pantry providing free supplemental pet food (up to 4 pets per household monthly) to local families in temporary financial hardship, to keep pets in their homes. Open Saturdays 8am-11am; requires referral/proof of need, photo ID. Renewable in 6-month periods.
records.free_claim: Free supplemental pet food for eligible families in temporary financial hardship who demonstrate need via referral/documentation (per org site).
records.nonprofit_claim: Five Acres Animal Shelter is a nonprofit organization.

records.name: Central Missouri Humane Society Pet Food Bank
records.what: Nonprofit humane society operating a free pet food bank for Boone County residents in financial hardship; food stocked by donations. Pets must be spayed/neutered to use it. Bring ID; call ahead to check availability.
records.free_claim: Service is free; pet food bank stocked by donations (site states free pickup for qualifying Boone County residents).
records.nonprofit_claim: 501(c)(3) nonprofit humane society (Federal Tax ID 43-0666742).

records.name: The Food Bank for Central & Northeast Missouri - Pet Food Distribution
records.what: Regional nonprofit food bank holds free pet food distributions (supported by community donations) at The Food Bank Market in Columbia, on a first-come, first-served basis. Serves the broader central/northeast Missouri region.
records.free_claim: Free pet food distribution supported by local donations; first-come, first-served (per Food Bank event page).
records.nonprofit_claim: Nonprofit regional food bank; member of Feeding America / Feeding Missouri network.
records.address: 705 Business Loop 70 W (The Food Bank Market)

records.name: HUGS Pet Food Pantry
records.what: Pet food pantry based in Huntsville (Randolph County, Northeast Missouri) listed on the Missouri Coalition of Animal Care Organizations directory as a pet-food assistance resource for owners in need. Recommend contacting directly for distribution hours/eligibility.
records.free_claim: Listed as a pet-food assistance (food bank) resource on the Missouri animal-care coalition pet-food directory.
records.nonprofit_claim: Listed among nonprofit/charitable pet-food assistance resources by the Missouri Coalition of Animal Care Organizations; formal nonprofit status not independently confirmed.

records.name: Northeast Missouri Humane Society - Pet Food Assistance
records.what: Nonprofit humane society in Hannibal serving Marion, Ralls, Monroe, Shelby, and Lewis counties; distributes pet food to pet-owning families in financial hardship to help prevent surrenders (reported 10,000+ lbs distributed). Contact directly for current pet-food assistance details and eligibility.
records.free_claim: Reported to distribute pet food at no charge to families in need to prevent pet surrenders; specific current program details/eligibility should be confirmed directly with the shelter.
records.nonprofit_claim: 501(c)(3) nonprofit humane society founded 1966 (GuideStar EIN 43-6063703).

records.name: Rescue One Pet Food Assistance Program
records.what: Nonprofit animal rescue offering temporary free pet food pickup to pet owners in need, as supply is available. In-person application required.
records.free_claim: Page states it provides temporary pet food assistance 'as food is available' at no cost; donations solicited to supply the program.
records.nonprofit_claim: Rescue One is a Springfield-based nonprofit animal rescue organization.

records.name: Snowflake's Snack Shack
records.what: Charitable pet food pantry providing free dog and cat food to low-income and homeless pet owners in the Joplin area. Distributes the second Saturday of each month, 11am-1pm.
records.free_claim: News coverage and findhelp listing describe it as a free pet food pantry providing food at no cost to those struggling to make ends meet; 828 lbs given to 308 owners in first year.
records.nonprofit_claim: Charitable community pet food pantry founded by Ann Leach (Ozark Center mental health professional); listed by Missouri Coalition of Animal Care Organizations as a pet food assistance resource.

records.name: Humane Society of Southwest Missouri - Pet Food Assistance
records.what: Humane society that partners with local food banks and pantries to supply pet food for distribution to their clients across the Springfield/Greene County area; clients inquire at their local pantry.
records.free_claim: HSSW partners with local food banks/pantries to provide pet food free to pantry clients (supply not guaranteed); listed on Missouri Coalition pet food assistance directory.
records.nonprofit_claim: Humane Society of Southwest Missouri is a registered nonprofit animal welfare organization / humane society in Springfield.

records.name: Paws Pet Pantry (Pets of the Homeless)
records.what: Pets of the Homeless affiliated donation/distribution site providing free pet food to homeless and low-income pet owners in the Springfield area, accessible by contacting the local site.
records.free_claim: Listed on Pets of the Homeless (national nonprofit) get-help locations directory as a pet food assistance site for people experiencing homelessness/hardship.
records.nonprofit_claim: Affiliated with Pets of the Homeless, a national 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing free pet food and care to the homeless and less fortunate.

records.name: The Crossing Nutrition Center
records.what: Faith-based nonprofit food pantry (a ministry of The Crossing, partner agency of Second Harvest) that provides free food including dog and cat food when available, to Buchanan County residents experiencing financial hardship.
records.free_claim: Provides food at no cost, including dog/cat food when available.
records.nonprofit_claim: Operated by The Crossing Outreach, a faith-based ministry / nonprofit; partner agency of Second Harvest Community Food Bank.

records.name: Helping Hands Humane Society - Pet Food Pantry
records.what: Walk-in pet food pantry at the humane society providing pet food for dogs and/or cats to Shawnee County residents in need; once every 6 months per household. Free-will donation suggested but not required; ID required.
records.free_claim: Free pet food (free-will donation suggested but not required) for residents in need.
records.nonprofit_claim: Helping Hands Humane Society Inc. is a nonprofit animal welfare organization / humane society.

records.name: Lawrence Humane Society - Pet Pantry
records.what: Walk-in pet food pantry at the humane society; primary site for Lawrence-area residents to access free pet food. Distributes roughly 5,000 lbs of pet food monthly. No documentation or proof of income required. Part of their Crisis Pet Retention program.
records.free_claim: Free pet food with no documentation or proof of income required.
records.nonprofit_claim: Lawrence Humane Society is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit humane society.

records.name: Riley County Humane Society - Emergency Pet Food Pantry
records.what: Twice-monthly drive-through emergency pet food pantry serving low-income pet owners in the Manhattan/Riley County area. By application (lists pet details); volunteers provide a one-time two-week supply of food per pet, up to 5 pets per household. Application online or in person at the event.
records.free_claim: Free emergency pet food so owners do not have to choose between feeding themselves or their pet.
records.nonprofit_claim: Riley County Humane Society is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, all-volunteer animal welfare organization (formed 1975).

records.name: Wichita Animal Action League (WAAL) Emergency Pet Food Bank
records.free_claim: Free emergency pet food handed out from the WAAL office; designed to supplement an owner's ability to feed their pet during hardship. Pets must be spayed/neutered after first visit.
records.nonprofit_claim: Wichita Animal Action League is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit animal rescue (Charity Navigator 4-star).
records.what: Nonprofit animal-rescue pet food bank giving free pet food to low-income owners; open Wednesdays 10am-4pm at the office. Also coordinates donated pet food to six community partner pantry sites in Sedgwick County.

records.name: WAAL Community Pet Food Banks (Sedgwick County partner sites)
records.free_claim: Free pet food distributed at six community partner pantries/ministries across Sedgwick County, supplied by WAAL since the COVID-19 era.
records.nonprofit_claim: Coordinated by Wichita Animal Action League (501(c)(3) nonprofit) in partnership with churches, food pantries, and ministries (e.g., Table of Hope, United Methodist Open Door, Lord's Diner, St. Patrick's Lord's Pantry, Dear Neighbor Ministries, Redeemer Church).
records.what: Network of six faith-based/community pantry sites in Sedgwick County that hand out free WAAL-supplied pet food alongside human food assistance. Sites: Table of Hope (1650 N Fairview, Thu 9:30am-1:30pm by appt), United Methodist Open Door (2130 E 21st St N), Lord's Diner (520 N Broadway, Sun dinner), St. Patrick's Lord's Pantry (2007 N Arkansas), Dear Neighbor Ministries (1329 S Bluffview, Hilltop residents), Redeemer Church (1900 W McCormick).

records.name: Saline County Senior Services Meals on Wheels - Pet Food & Healthcare Program
records.free_claim: Free pet food, treats, supplies, and pet healthcare assistance delivered to homebound Meals on Wheels seniors so they don't share their own meals with their pets; funded by donations and a Meals on Wheels America grant.
records.nonprofit_claim: Run by Saline County Department of Senior Services (county/government agency) Meals on Wheels program, with funding via the Greater Salina Community Foundation and Meals on Wheels America (nonprofit). Listed as a nonprofit on Match Madness/GSCF.
records.what: Meals on Wheels pet program delivering free pet food and pet healthcare support to at-risk seniors in Salina / Saline County (central Kansas).
